ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "states" (
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each state */,
  "state_name" TEXT /* Name of the state */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the countries table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region (e.g., Appalachian Region) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the region, including demographic characteristics */,
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the states table */,
  "latitude" TEXT /* Geographic latitude of the region */,
  "longitude" TEXT /* Geographic longitude of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "counties" (
  "county_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each county */,
  "county_name" TEXT /* Name of the county */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the regions table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("county_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "economic_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each economic data entry */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the recorded data */,
  "county_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the counties table */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of economic metric (e.g., population, GDP, unemployment rate) */,
  "metric_value" TEXT /* Value of the economic metric */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the economic data */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for data creation */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for data updates */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* User's password (hashed for security) */,
  "role" TEXT /* User's role (e.g., admin, analyst, guest) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the users table */,
  "permission" TEXT /* Specific permission granted to the user (e.g., view_data, edit_data, delete_data) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "permission")
)
column_names: Foreign key referencing the countries table

Description of the region, including demographic characteristics
